SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol
Interface MediaEvent<S>

All Known Subinterfaces:
AllocationEvent, JoinEvent, MixerEvent, PlayerEvent, RecorderEvent, ResourceEvent<T>, SdpPortManagerEvent, SignalDetectorEvent, SignalGeneratorEvent, SpeechRecognitionEvent, VideoRendererEvent, VxmlDialogEvent
public interface MediaEvent<S>

An event from a MediaEventNotifier. Characterized by the source of the event, and an EventType indicating its nature.

Field Summary
static MediaErr NO_ERROR
          Error returned by getError() when there is no error.
 
Method Summary
 MediaErr getError()
          Identify the reason or cause of an error or failure.
 java.lang.String getErrorText()
          Returns a human-readable error cause, if any.
 EventType getEventType()
          Get the EventType that identifies the event nature.
 S getSource()
          Gives access to the source of the Media Event.
 boolean isSuccessful()
           
 

Field Detail

NO_ERROR

static final MediaErr NO_ERROR
Error returned by getError() when there is no error.

Method Detail

getSource

S getSource()
Gives access to the source of the Media Event. Can be for example: Player , SdpPortManager, MediaMixer or VxmlDialog

getEventType

EventType getEventType()
Get the EventType that identifies the event nature.

For completion events, this identifies the operation that has completed. It should never return null, even in the case of an error event.
Further detail about the reason for this event is available using ResourceEvent.getQualifier().

Returns:
the EventType identifying the event nature
See Also:
ResourceEvent

isSuccessful

boolean isSuccessful()

getError

MediaErr getError()
Identify the reason or cause of an error or failure.

If this Event is associated with an error, then getError() returns a MediaErr that identifies the problem.
If this Event is not associated with an error, then getError() returns MediaErr.NO_ERROR.

The list of generic error is defined in class MediaErr. Resources can define more specific errors.

Returns:
a MediaErr

getErrorText

java.lang.String getErrorText()
Returns a human-readable error cause, if any.
Enables implementors to provide additional information.
